ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT ARVINMERITOR INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ARGUED AND THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT ACTED WITH THE REQUISITE MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION TO WARRANT THE IMPOSITION OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SPECIFICALLY THE PORTION OF PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSE THAT DISCUSSES AND CITES TO BRUCE KETCHAM'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY, PRECLUDE DEFENDANT FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. EVEN IF DEFENDANT HAD SUSTAINED ITS BURDEN, PLAINTIFFS PRESENTED EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT ACTED WITH THE REQUISITE MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION TO WARRANT THE IMPOSITION OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL DOES NOT APPLY. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE ISSUES DECIDED IN BANKHEAD V. ARVINMERITOR, INC., (2012) 205 CAL.APP.4TH 68, ARE IDENTICAL WITH THE ONES PRESENTED HERE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: RHONDA HARRIS, CSR #7414

ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT NACCO MATERIALS HANDLING GROUP INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT NACCO MATERIALS HANDLING GROUP, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT NACCO MATERIALS HANDLING GROUP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS OFF CALENDAR. THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF MR. BANGS AND MR. LIBBY CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. LEPORE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT FAILED TO PROPERLY MAKE AN ISSUE OF SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR CAUSATION, BUT EVEN IF IT HAD, DR. HORN'S DECLARATION WOULD CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. LEPORE'S EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING OR CONTRIBUTING TO HIS ASBESTOS-RELATED ILLNESS. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT THE SOPHISTICATED USER DEFENSE APPLIES. THE NAVY'S KNOWLEDGE OF THE HAZARDS OF ASBESTOS CANNOT BE IMPUTED TO MR. LEPORE. PLAINTIFFS DISMISSED THEIR CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: PATRICIA RODRIGUEZ OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-22-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth R. Bain of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9513), appearing for defendant / moving party NAACCO MATERIALS HANDLING GROUP, INC.
Stephen Tigerman of HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN, LLP (415-788-1588) appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Gene Lepore

RULING:
DEFENDANT NACCO MATERIALS HANDLING GROUP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Nacco Materials Handling Group, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is off calendar. The deposition testimony of Mr. Bangs and Mr. Libby create a triable issue whether Mr. Lepore was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's separate statement failed to properly make an issue of substantial factor causation, but even if it had, Dr. Horn's declaration would create a triable issue whether Mr. Lepore's exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his asbestos-related illness. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies. The Navy's knowledge of the hazards of asbestos cannot be imputed to Mr. Lepore. Plaintiffs dismissed their claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Mia Mattis of HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN, LLP (415-788-1588) appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Gene Lepore

RULING:
DEFENDANT DEERE & COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Deere & Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Lepore was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories (defendant's Exhibit S) and the deposition testimony of Mr. Bangs, Mr. Bailey, Mr. Villegas and Mr. Roman preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant's moving papers failed to properly make an issue of substantial factor causation. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jul-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ARVINMERITOR INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES.
Appearances: Stephen Tigerman of Harowitz & Tigerman, LLP (788.1588) for plaintiff and responding party; Michelle C. Jackson of McKenna Long & Aldridge LLP (267.4132) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court ruled as follows: denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories, specifically the portion of plaintiffs' response that discusses and cites to Bruce Ketcham's deposition testimony, preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, plaintiffs presented evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Collateral estoppel does not apply. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate that the issues decided in Bankhead v. ArvinMeritor, Inc., (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 68, are identical with the ones presented here. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Rhonda Harris, CSR #7414 (503/TLJ)
